title: IO::Buffer#& use-after-free when self or mask is an invalidated slice
category: use-after-free (stale slice base pointer across the Ruby-object ↔ C-buffer boundary)
crash_site: SIGSEGV / heap-use-after-free reading a buffer base pointer inside io_buffer.c during IO::Buffer#& (bitwise AND). The operator code is correct; the memory it reads was already released.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: io_buffer_free / IO::Buffer#free (the actual invalidation, performed elsewhere) and the sibling operators io_buffer_or / io_buffer_xor. The crash is in io_buffer_and but the freeing happened earlier on the parent buffer.
ground_truth.fix_summary: io_buffer_and read buffer->base and mask_buffer->base directly without checking the buffers were still live; a slice whose parent had been freed kept a stale base pointer -> UAF. Fix: use io_buffer_get_bytes_for_reading for both operands, which raises IO::Buffer::InvalidatedError before any memory access.

admission.note: Authored from PR #16964 body+diff. crash != cause: the SEGV is in io_buffer_and reading buffer->base, but the cause is the parent buffer freed elsewhere (IO::Buffer#free) leaving the slice's base dangling; io_buffer_and failed to validate liveness the way other read paths do (io_buffer_get_bytes_for_reading). Single file -> if a hard baseline greps io_buffer.c to FULL, down-weight. Ruby slice lifetime <-> C base pointer. Run baseline first.

provenance: https://github.com/ruby/ruby/pull/16964 — Fix UAF in IO::Buffer#& when self or mask is an invalidated slice; fix 2552db04ddc4, base 356c0cd0e795.
